evals.prompt: Our involuntary churn is 4% per month. Is dunning the problem and where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Pulls current recovery rates by attempt and reason code before answering. Splits churn into voluntary and involuntary to confirm the framing. Identifies dunning as a high-leverage lever; cites that involuntary often represents 25-40% of total churn for subscription brands. Walks through the diagnostic and the canonical retry sequence.
assertions: Calls modern.subscriptions.dunning_recovery to baseline recovery
Calls modern.subscriptions.churn_rate split voluntary vs involuntary
Cites that involuntary often represents 25-40% of total churn
Identifies dunning as a high-leverage lever for the diagnosis
Walks through the canonical retry sequence
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Right now we retry the card 5 times in 24 hours and then cancel. Is that aggressive?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the 5-in-24h pattern as wrong. The right schedule is sparser and stretches over 14 days: immediate, +24h, +72h, +7d, +14d. Rapid back-to-back retries on the same day rarely recover NSF cases (account hasn't been replenished) and trigger more bank-side flags. Walks through the recommended schedule.
assertions: Identifies the 5-in-24h pattern as wrong
Recommends the +24h / +72h / +7d / +14d spacing
Names that rapid retries rarely recover NSF
Names the bank-side flag risk of rapid retries
Recommends silent attempt 1
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: How do I tell the difference between a customer whose card expired vs one whose account was overdrawn?
evals.expected_output: Names the importance of reason-code segmentation. Walks through the main categories (NSF, expired card, network decline, blocked/fraud, lost/stolen, limit exceeded) and the different intervention per code. Notes that a reason-aware sequence outperforms a unified sequence by 20-40% in recovered revenue.
assertions: Calls modern.subscriptions.dunning_recovery grouped by reason_code
Walks through the main reason-code categories
Names different intervention per code
Names that NSF responds to retry; expired card requires update prompt
Cites the 20-40% reason-aware vs unified gap
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We send the same payment-failed email at retry 1, retry 2, and retry 3. Is that wrong?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the single-template approach as wrong. Recommends silent attempt 1, operational copy at attempt 2, SMS+email at attempt 3, and 'last attempt' framing at attempt 4. Walks through the payment-method-update prompt design (operational language, low friction, magic-link tap-to-update). Cites SMS 3-5x conversion lift on opted-in customers.
assertions: Identifies single-template approach as wrong
Recommends silent attempt 1, escalating channel at each step
Recommends SMS at attempt 3
Cites SMS 3-5x conversion lift on opted-in customers
Recommends operational copy over apologetic or promotional
Recommends magic-link or tap-to-update over multi-step forms
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Can I prevent payment failures before they happen?
evals.expected_output: Walks through pre-failure prevention framework. Expiration warning at 30/14/3 days before expiration. Card-updater services (VAU, ABU) auto-refresh replaced card numbers at processor level. Pre-failure prevention is structurally the highest-leverage move. Recommends checking whether card-updater is enabled and adding the expiration-warning sequence.
assertions: Recommends expiration warning at 30/14/3 days before expiration
Names card-updater services (VAU, ABU)
Names pre-failure prevention as structurally highest-leverage
Recommends verifying card-updater is enabled
Cites the 5-10% of involuntary churn recoverable with no customer involvement
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: When should we just give up and cancel a subscription after failed payments?
evals.expected_output: Recommends hard stop at attempt 4 (+7 days) for most categories. Names pause-do-not-cancel as the right transition. Walks through reactivation flow at days 14/30/60 post-pause as separate from dunning copy. Names suppression criteria for customers with hostile feedback or repeat failed cycles, cross-referencing winback-flows and nps-and-detractor-handling do-not-contact rules.
assertions: Recommends hard stop at attempt 4 (+7 days) for most categories
Recommends pausing the subscription rather than cancelling
Names the reactivation flow as separate from dunning flow
Names suppression criteria for hostile/repeat-failure customers
Cross-references winback-flows do-not-contact rules
Cross-references nps-and-detractor-handling do-not-contact rules
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Should I let customers add a backup payment method like ACH?
evals.expected_output: Strongly recommends adding secondary-method backup capture. Cites typical 15-30% involuntary churn reduction. Walks through capture timing: at subscription start, at first card update, at pause/skip events. Explains the mechanic of friction reduction at the worst moment for the customer.
assertions: Strongly recommends adding secondary-method backup
Cites the 15-30% involuntary churn reduction
Names capture timing (subscription start, first card update, pause/skip)
Explains the friction-reduction mechanic
Names ACH, SEPA, or second credit card as options
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We retry every 12 hours regardless of day. Does retry timing within the week matter?
evals.expected_output: Walks through smart-retry timing. End-of-month (28th-31st) has highest decline rate; retries scheduled for 1st-3rd of next month recover more. Avoid weekend-night settlement windows; avoid bank holidays. Recommends verifying whether the brand's processor has smart-retry features enabled (Stripe smart retries, Recharge intelligent retry).
assertions: Names end-of-month (28th-31st) as highest decline rate window
Recommends retry scheduled for 1st-3rd of next month for NSF
Names weekend-night settlement window avoidance
Names holiday/bank-closure avoidance
Recommends verifying processor smart-retry is enabled
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections
